title: OSMM Product Context Object
description: Durable, foundational Context describing the thing being marketed: what an offering is, what it does, how it works, the customer outcomes it delivers, and its product-level messaging. Canonical schema for object_type 'product_context'. The osmm-product-context-builder skill references this file; it is the single source of truth for the object's shape.

osmm_version.description: OSMM schema version this instance conforms to (semantic version).

product_id.description: Stable, human-readable id. Prefix 'PRD-' + lowercase hyphen slug. Never changes once assigned.

version.description: Instance version (e.g. '1.0'). Bump on revision; the id stays stable.

offering_type.description: Governed offering-type vocabulary. Pick the single closest value.

offering_category.description: Plain-language 'what kind of thing it is' (not a taxonomy code).

tagline.description: The offering's own short line, if it has a recognizable one. Optional.

description.description: 2-3 neutral, factual sentences: what it is, who it's for, the job it does.

what_it_does.description: One to three sentences on the core function — the job the offering performs.

how_it_works.description: Key mechanics / components / steps that deliver the value. Substance, not brochure copy.

benefits.description: Customer outcomes phrased as benefits, not features.

use_cases.description: Primary jobs-to-be-done / scenarios the offering addresses.

differentiators.description: Product-level distinction vs. alternatives, grounded in evidence.

product_messaging.description: The durable product-level messaging — the source for downstream creative/messaging.

primary_message.description: The single thing to land about the offering.

proof_points.description: Evidence substantiating the claims (stats, certifications, named customers, awards).

objections.description: Known buyer pushback and the counter, each { objection, response }. Optional.

pricing_model.description: How it's priced at all (subscription, per-seat, tiered, menu price). A promotion is an Offer, not this. Optional.

tiers_or_variants.description: Editions, sizes, configurations, or SKUs if relevant. Optional.

target_segments.description: Plain descriptions of who it's for; structure lives in linked Audience/Persona objects. Optional.

lifecycle_stage.description: Where the offering sits in its lifecycle. Optional.

linked_business_context.description: Id of the Business Context that offers it. Use 'BIZ-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' until it exists.

linked_brand_context.description: Id of the Brand Context if marketed under a specific brand. 'BRC-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' ok. Optional.

related_offerings.description: 'PRD-' ids of complementary, parent, or bundled offerings. 'PRD-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' ok. Optional.

source.description: One line: what source(s) this was built from and approximate date. Optional.
